feed.title: Default signet reorg history — was the fork at height 287766 (depth 19) deliberate?

entry.title: Default signet reorg history — was the fork at height 287766 (depth 19) deliberate?
entry.summary:
The discussion involves the examination of blockchain reorganizations, particularly focusing on their frequency and depth. It is noted that moderately deep reorganizations, specifically those around 6 to 10 blocks deep, have occurred on Signet. Two specific instances are mentioned, one involving a 7-block reorg and another a 9-block reorg. These instances raise questions regarding whether they were intentional as part of the network design or if they stemmed from operational issues similar to those observed in a previously mentioned 19-block reorg incident.
Understanding the nature of these reorgs is crucial for assessing blockchain stability and security. A clear differentiation between designed reorganization events and those caused by operational challenges helps in evaluating the robustness of blockchain protocols. Such insights are pivotal for developers and network engineers in improving and fortifying blockchain infrastructure.
